designer.title: NexUI Designer
component.label: Text / Label
message.motionGraphNoPreset: Select an element with a Motion Preset assigned to edit its motion graph.
validation.missingContractElement: A required contract element is missing.
validation.missingGlyph: The font is missing a required glyph.
tooltip.variants: Create and edit variants that open the same screen differently per situation (Compact, Controller, Combat, ...).
tooltip.responsive: Edit responsive rules that adapt layout automatically by resolution and input mode.
tooltip.contract: Define and check the elements and capabilities a screen must provide.
tooltip.snapshot: Capture the current UI state as a snapshot and compare it later to detect regressions.
tooltip.diff: Show element/binding/motion/theme/policy differences before vs after in a readable form.
tooltip.refactor: Safely rename identifiers (elementId, screenId, stateKey, ...) including all references.
tooltip.cleaner: Find dead references (broken bindings, keys, targets) and remove or replace them.
tooltip.flowSimulator: Simulate interaction flow from button click through command execution and screen transitions.
tooltip.inputPreview: Preview UI differences per input mode: Keyboard/Mouse, Gamepad, Touch, SteamDeck.
tooltip.promptGlyph: Manage per-device button prompt glyphs and check for missing ones.
tooltip.gameLocalization: Edit the ko-KR/en-US game UI text table and check for missing translations / overflow.
tooltip.fontChecker: Check whether the current font can render Korean / special glyphs and whether TMP fallback is set.
tooltip.profiler: Measure opened screen/element/binding/motion counts and open/close/theme-apply timings.
tooltip.bindingProfiler: Track binding update frequency and cost, warning on excessively updated keys.
tooltip.motionBudget: Limit concurrent motions and manage budget overruns and priorities.
tooltip.contrastChecker: Compute WCAG contrast for theme color pairs and warn on low-readability combinations.
tooltip.loadingStrategy: Set each screen's load strategy (Preload, LazyLoad, Addressable, Pool, KeepAlive).
tooltip.recipes: Generate common UI structures (Pause, Settings, Inventory, ...) from templates in one step.
tooltip.agentHandoff: Export the current project state as JSON/Markdown for an AI CLI agent to understand.
tooltip.accessibility: Preview accessibility preferences (reduce motion, high contrast, large text) and their effects.
tooltip.captureSnapshot: Capture the current screen's element state as a snapshot.
tooltip.compare: Compute and show the difference between the two selected targets.
tooltip.rename: Rename all references together to the entered name.
tooltip.findDeadReferences: Search the whole project for broken references.
tooltip.clean: Remove or replace the detected broken references.
tooltip.simulate: Run the interaction flow of the selected element and show the result.
tooltip.exportManifest: Export the AI handoff Manifest (JSON) and Brief (Markdown) to files.
tooltip.copyCliPrompt: Copy a prompt for the CLI agent to the clipboard.
tooltip.toolbar.screen: The UIScreenDefinition asset to edit. Opening a screen rebuilds the live preview from its backend asset (UXML/prefab).
tooltip.toolbar.metadata: The DesignerMetadataAsset holding this screen's element layout/bindings/motion/theme data. Click New to create one for the open screen.
tooltip.toolbar.newMetadata: Creates a new DesignerMetadataAsset for the currently open Screen and assigns it above.
tooltip.toolbar.status: Backend | open Screen id, colored by validation status (green = no issues, orange = warnings/errors present).
tooltip.toolbar.modeToggle: Simple mode hides advanced-only sections (Theme token overrides, Motion, screen Policy, raw Capability info, and the extra Binding keys beyond Command). Advanced reveals everything. Remembered per-user.
tooltip.toolbar.preview: Live canvas resolution preset (C1: responsive breakpoint preview) - switch between phone/tablet/desktop/Steam Deck sizes without leaving the Designer.
tooltip.toolbar.state: Preview a per-state Motion variant (e.g. Hover/Pressed/Focus Variant on the Motion inspector) without needing to trigger it live.
tooltip.toolbar.input: Preview device-specific prompt glyphs/behavior for this input mode.
tooltip.toolbar.snap: Snaps move/resize/create operations to the grid size below.
tooltip.toolbar.rebuild: Rebuilds the live preview from scratch (backend elements + bindings + theme + motion) - use if the canvas looks out of sync with the metadata.
tooltip.toolbar.save: Saves the Designer metadata and the open Screen asset.
tooltip.toolbar.validate: Re-runs all project validators (duplicate ids, missing assets, backend mismatches, ...) against the open screen.
tooltip.toolbar.distributeH: Space 3+ selected elements evenly along the horizontal axis.
tooltip.toolbar.distributeV: Space 3+ selected elements evenly along the vertical axis.
tooltip.toolbar.bringForward: Move the selected element one step forward (toward the front) in draw order.
tooltip.toolbar.sendBackward: Move the selected element one step backward (toward the back) in draw order.
tooltip.toolbar.bringToFront: Move the selected element to the very front of the draw order.
tooltip.toolbar.sendToBack: Move the selected element to the very back of the draw order.
tooltip.style.id: Unique id used by binding keys, commandKeys, and backend name-matching (UXML/GameObject name). Renaming this repoints child parentIds automatically.
tooltip.style.displayName: Human-readable label shown in the Hierarchy panel. Purely cosmetic - does not affect runtime lookup.
tooltip.style.type: Semantic element type (Panel, Button, Label, ...). Drives the default backend visual and which capabilities the runtime expects it to expose.
tooltip.style.text: Static preview text for Label/Button-like elements. Overridden at runtime by binding.textKey if one is set.
tooltip.style.classes: Space-separated style classes applied to this element (USS classes for UI Toolkit, or the equivalent class-toggle set for uGUI).
tooltip.style.shape: Designer-canvas preview shape for this element's box: Rectangle (square corners), Rounded (small radius, the previous default), Pill (fully rounded ends), or Circle/Ellipse. Preview only - does not change the backend UXML/prefab's own styling.
tooltip.style.previewValue: Fill amount shown in the Designer canvas for this Progress Bar/Stat Bar/Radial Fill, in the Min/Max range below (preview only - the real value at runtime comes from binding.valueKey).
tooltip.style.minValue: Lower bound of the preview value range, matching Unity Slider's minValue (e.g. 0).
tooltip.style.maxValue: Upper bound of the preview value range, matching Unity Slider's maxValue (e.g. 100).
tooltip.style.fillDirection: Which edge the fill grows from, matching Unity Image's FillMethod + fillOrigin (Horizontal/Left,Right or Vertical/Bottom,Top).
tooltip.style.clockwise: Sweep direction of the radial fill/spin arc, matching Unity Image's fillClockwise.
tooltip.style.previewImage: Texture shown in the Designer canvas for this Image/Icon Button (preview only - the backend UXML/prefab keeps its own sprite/texture reference).
tooltip.style.tint: Background/graphic tint color shown in the Designer preview and applied to the backend element.
tooltip.style.fontSize: Font size in points, clamped to 8-96.
tooltip.style.hidden: Hides this element in the Designer canvas only (does not affect runtime visibility) - use to declutter the canvas without deleting metadata.
tooltip.layout.position: Top-left position in canvas pixels (absolute, not relative to the parent element).
tooltip.layout.size: Width/height in canvas pixels. Clamped to a 24px minimum on each axis.
tooltip.layout.anchor: uGUI-style anchor preset applied to the backend RectTransform/style on save (Stretch fills the parent on that axis).
tooltip.layout.locked: Prevents move/resize/rect edits on the canvas (Inspector fields still work) - use to avoid accidentally dragging a finished element.
tooltip.binding.textKey: UIStateStore key whose string value drives this element's text at runtime (via UITextBinder). Leave empty for static text.
tooltip.binding.valueKey: UIStateStore key whose float value drives this element's Value capability at runtime (progress bars, sliders).
tooltip.binding.visibilityKey: UIStateStore key whose bool value shows/hides this element at runtime.
tooltip.binding.classKey: UIStateStore key whose string value is applied as a style class at runtime (for state-driven styling, e.g. "selected"/"error").
tooltip.binding.commandKey: Action key executed when this element is clicked - resolved through a UIActionResolver at runtime (see UICommandBinder). Use Pick... to reuse a key already wired elsewhere instead of typing a new one.
tooltip.binding.interactableKey: UIStateStore key whose bool value enables/disables interaction on this element at runtime.
tooltip.binding.pickData: Connect this binding key to a data-source IBindableProperty<T> found by reflection.
tooltip.binding.pickCommand: Choose from built-in pipeline commands or commandKeys already used elsewhere in this project.
tooltip.theme.themeAsset: Optional direct theme asset reference. Assigning one also seeds Theme Id below from the asset's themeId, if empty.
tooltip.theme.themeId: Theme id string used to resolve theme tokens at runtime, independent of the direct asset reference above (useful when the theme is swapped at runtime via NexUITheme.Use).
tooltip.theme.classes: Space-separated theme-specific style classes, applied in addition to the Style section's Classes field.
tooltip.theme.addToken: Overrides a single theme token (e.g. "color.primary") for this element only, without touching the shared theme asset.
tooltip.theme.eyedropperColor: Click the swatch to open Unity's color picker - it has a built-in eyedropper to sample any pixel on screen. Then press "Add as Token".
tooltip.theme.addEyedropperToken: Adds the color on the left as a new token override (name the key afterward).
tooltip.theme.tokenValue: Override value for this token, on this element only.
tooltip.motion.preset: The compiled motion asset (built with the Motion Graph editor) this element animates with.
tooltip.motion.motionId: Identifier used to look up this motion at runtime; seeded from the preset's own motionId when one is assigned.
tooltip.motion.initial: Variant name applied the instant this element becomes visible (before any animation plays).
tooltip.motion.animate: Variant name played when the element enters (e.g. screen open).
tooltip.motion.exit: Variant name played when the element exits (e.g. screen close).
tooltip.motion.hover: Variant name played on pointer hover, if the backend/capability supports it.
tooltip.motion.pressed: Variant name played while the element is pressed/held.
tooltip.motion.focus: Variant name played while the element has keyboard/gamepad focus.
tooltip.motion.openGraph: Opens the visual node-graph editor for the assigned preset.
tooltip.motion.openClipEditor: Opens the timeline clip editor scoped to this specific element in the live preview.
tooltip.policy.inputBlocking: Blocks pointer/gamepad input to screens behind this one while it's open.
tooltip.policy.pauseGameBehind: Pauses gameplay (Time.timeScale-style) while this screen is open.
tooltip.policy.closeOnBack: Closes this screen when the Back command (Cancel/Escape) fires.
tooltip.policy.cursorPolicy: Cursor visibility/lock state to force while this screen is open (Unchanged leaves whatever the game already set).
tooltip.policy.timePolicy: Whether opening this screen pauses or slows game time.
tooltip.policy.focusPolicy: Keyboard/gamepad focus behavior on open - AutoFocusDefault focuses focus.defaultFocusElementId; TrapFocus additionally prevents Tab from leaving the screen.
tooltip.policy.conflictPolicy: What happens if this screen is asked to open/close again while a transition is already playing.
tooltip.policy.lifetimePolicy: Whether the screen instance is destroyed, kept alive, or pooled when closed.
tooltip.accessibility.label: Screen-reader-facing label for this element. Falls back to Text/Name at runtime when empty.
tooltip.accessibility.role: Semantic role hint (Button, Toggle, Slider, ...) for screen-reader/switch-control bridges, following the common ARIA/UIKit role vocabulary.
tooltip.autoLayout.enabled: Arranges this element's direct children along one axis (Figma-style Auto Layout), instead of each child keeping an independent absolute rect.
tooltip.autoLayout.direction: Axis children are arranged along - Row (left to right) or Column (top to bottom).
tooltip.autoLayout.spacing: Gap between consecutive children along the direction axis, in pixels.
tooltip.autoLayout.paddingLeft: Inset from this element's left edge to its children.
tooltip.autoLayout.paddingTop: Inset from this element's top edge to its children.
tooltip.autoLayout.paddingRight: Inset from this element's right edge to its children.
tooltip.autoLayout.paddingBottom: Inset from this element's bottom edge to its children.
tooltip.autoLayout.widthSizing: How this element sizes horizontally when its parent is an Auto Layout container: Fixed (use its own rect width), Hug (fit content), or Fill (grow to take remaining space).
tooltip.autoLayout.heightSizing: How this element sizes vertically when its parent is an Auto Layout container: Fixed (use its own rect height), Hug (fit content), or Fill (grow to take remaining space).
tooltip.constraints.horizontal: Pin behavior on the horizontal axis when the parent is resized: Start (left), End (right), Center, or Scale (resize proportionally).
tooltip.constraints.vertical: Pin behavior on the vertical axis when the parent is resized: Start (top), End (bottom), Center, or Scale (resize proportionally).
tooltip.capability.list: Runtime capability interfaces (IUI*Capability) this element type is expected to expose - informational, derived from the Type field in the Style section.
tooltip.multiSelection.summary: Number of elements in the current multi-selection.
tooltip.multiSelection.position: Shared position across the selection, or "Mixed" if they differ.
tooltip.multiSelection.size: Shared size across the selection, or "Mixed" if they differ.
tooltip.multiSelection.layer: Draw-order index range covered by the selection (contiguous elements only), or "Mixed" otherwise.
tooltip.validation.text: This element's data is checked against these four categories whenever validation runs - see the Validation panel below for the actual issue list.
tooltip.screenDefinition.screen: The UIScreenDefinition asset currently open (read-only here - use the toolbar's Screen field to switch).
tooltip.palette.search: Filter the component list by name.
tooltip.palette.addComponent: Add a {0} element to the current screen.
tooltip.palette.alignLeft: Align the selected element's left edge to the canvas.
tooltip.palette.alignCenterX: Center the selected element horizontally on the canvas.
tooltip.palette.alignRight: Align the selected element's right edge to the canvas.
tooltip.palette.alignTop: Align the selected element's top edge to the canvas.
tooltip.palette.alignCenterY: Center the selected element vertically on the canvas.
tooltip.palette.alignBottom: Align the selected element's bottom edge to the canvas.
tooltip.palette.fill: Resize the selected element to fill the entire canvas resolution.
tooltip.palette.copy: Duplicate the selected element(s), offset slightly from the original.
tooltip.palette.delete: Delete the selected element(s). This does not delete their children - reparent those first if needed.
tooltip.hierarchy.search: Filter elements by id or display name.
tooltip.hierarchy.visibility: Toggle visibility in the Designer canvas (does not affect runtime visibility).
tooltip.hierarchy.lock: Toggle locked (prevents move/resize/rect edits on the canvas).
tooltip.state.title: Lists every UIStateStore binding key wired on the selected element (or every element, if none is selected).
tooltip.state.filter: Filter the binding key list below by substring.
tooltip.state.keys: elementId / binding kind = UIStateStore key, for every binding on the selected element (or all elements).
tooltip.command.title: Preview/edit the selected element's commandKey (the action dispatched when it's clicked at runtime).
tooltip.command.field: Command key for the selected element. Editing here is the same as the Binding inspector's Command Key field.
tooltip.command.run: Preview-dispatch this command key (does not actually invoke the runtime Command Pipeline - just previews the key that would be sent) and save it onto the selected element.
tooltip.screenGraph.title: C1 command/binding link view: with nothing selected, summarizes the screen (backend, variant/responsive-rule/element counts); with an element selected, shows exactly which commandKey/textKey/valueKey/etc. it's wired to.
tooltip.validationPanel.title: Results from every registered project validator (duplicate ids, missing assets, backend mismatches, ...), re-run on Validate or whenever the metadata changes.
tooltip.validationPanel.item: Click to select "{0}" in the Hierarchy/canvas.
